common_mistakes: Arching the back, holding breath, rushing the movement
description: The Hundred is a foundational Pilates exercise that warms up the body, engages the core, and stimulates circulation.
form_tips: Keep your abs drawn in, maintain a neutral spine, breathe rhythmically with the arm pumps
instructions: 1. Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat. 2. Lift your head and shoulders off the mat. 3. Extend legs to a 45-degree angle. 4. Pump arms up and down while inhaling for 5 counts and exhaling for 5 counts. 5. Complete 10 sets (100 counts total).
name: Shoulders
column16: For beginners: Keep knees bent at 90 degrees. For advanced: Lower legs closer to the mat while maintaining proper form.
column17: https://example.com/videos/hundred.mp4

common_mistakes: Rushing the rolling motion, straining the neck, using momentum instead of control
description: Roll Up is a precise movement that strengthens the abdominals while stretching the back and hamstrings.
form_tips: Focus on articulating through each vertebra, keep shoulders relaxed and away from ears, use control rather than momentum
instructions: 1. Lie on your back with arms extended overhead. 2. Inhale to prepare. 3. Exhale as you roll up one vertebra at a time, reaching toward your toes. 4. Inhale at the top. 5. Exhale as you roll back down with control.
name: Spinal Extensors
column16: Beginners can bend knees slightly or use hands behind thighs for assistance. Advanced practitioners can add a pause at the top or perform with straight legs and arms overhead.
column17: https://example.com/videos/rollup.mp4

common_mistakes: Lifting shoulders, tucking chin too much, using arm strength instead of abdominal control
description: Single Leg Circles strengthen the abdominals while improving hip mobility and leg control.
form_tips: Keep your pelvis stable throughout the movement, maintain a neutral spine, and focus on using your abdominals to control the movement rather than momentum
instructions: 1. Lie on your back with one leg extended to the ceiling and the other leg extended along the mat. 2. Circle the raised leg in a controlled manner, making a small circle. 3. Perform 5-10 circles in one direction, then reverse.
name: Hip Flexors
column17: Beginners can bend the stationary leg or make smaller circles. Advanced practitioners can perform larger circles or add ankle weights.

common_mistakes: Collapsing chest, dropping head, arching low back
description: Rolling Like a Ball massages the spine, develops balance, and strengthens the abdominals.
form_tips: Find your balance point, keep elbows wide, maintain a C-curve shape throughout
instructions: 1. Sit with knees bent, feet off the mat, and hands holding behind thighs. 2. Round your back into a C-curve. 3. Inhale as you roll back to your shoulder blades. 4. Exhale as you roll back up to the starting position, without letting your feet touch the mat.
name: Spinal Flexors
column16: Beginners can place hands on ankles for more support. Advanced practitioners can place hands behind head or extend arms forward.
column17: https://example.com/videos/rolling_like_a_ball.mp4

common_mistakes: Raising shoulders, collapsing chest, not engaging abdominals properly
description: Single Leg Stretch targets the abdominal muscles while improving coordination and breath control.
form_tips: Keep shoulder blades on the mat, maintain a stable pelvis, and coordinate breath with movement
instructions: 1. Lie on your back, lift head and shoulders off the mat. 2. Bring both knees toward chest. 3. Extend one leg while pulling the other knee in with both hands. 4. Switch legs in a fluid motion, coordinating with breath.
name: Hip Flexors
column16: Beginners can keep head and shoulders on the mat. Advanced practitioners can lift higher or extend legs lower to increase intensity.
column17: https://example.com/videos/single_leg_stretch.mp4

common_mistakes: Allowing hips to rock, dropping knees to sides, holding breath
description: Double Leg Stretch intensifies core engagement while coordinating arm and leg movements.
form_tips: Keep lower back pressed into mat, maintain stable core throughout, coordinate breath with movement pattern
instructions: 1. Lie on back with knees to chest, head and shoulders lifted. 2. Inhale as you extend arms overhead and legs out to 45 degrees. 3. Exhale as you circle arms around and return to starting position.
name: Hip Flexors
column17: Intermediate practitioners can bring legs higher or bend knees slightly. Beginners should master Single Leg Stretch first.

common_mistakes: Lifting shoulders toward ears, twisting from shoulders instead of waist, rushing the movement
description: Spine Twist strengthens obliques and rotational core muscles while improving spinal mobility.
form_tips: Sit tall with a lengthened spine, initiate the twist from your waist not shoulders, keep hips anchored throughout
instructions: 1. Sit tall with legs extended in front, slightly apart. 2. Extend arms out to sides at shoulder height. 3. Inhale to prepare, then exhale as you twist torso to one side. 4. Inhale to center, then exhale to twist to the other side.
name: Spinal Rotators
column16: Beginners can perform with crossed legs. Advanced practitioners can perform with legs together or raised slightly off mat.
column17: https://example.com/videos/spine_twist.mp4

common_mistakes: Collapsing chest, hunching shoulders, hyperextending spine
description: Swimming strengthens the entire posterior chain while developing coordination and back extension.
form_tips: Keep neck long, engage lower abdominals to support lower back, focus on length rather than height
instructions: 1. Lie on stomach with arms extended overhead and legs straight. 2. Lift arms, chest, and legs off mat. 3. Flutter opposite arm and leg in a controlled swimming motion. 4. Breathe normally throughout.
name: Glutes
column17: Intermediate practitioners can lift just arms or just legs. Beginners should master single limb lifts before attempting.

common_mistakes: Holding breath, using momentum, losing core engagement
description: Teaser is one of the most challenging Pilates exercises, requiring exceptional core strength and control.
form_tips: Keep your chest open, shoulders down, and focus on using your deep abdominals to control the movement
instructions: 1. Lie on back with legs extended at 45 degrees, arms overhead. 2. Inhale to prepare. 3. Exhale as you roll up, bringing arms parallel to legs. 4. Hold in V-position. 5. Roll down with control.
name: Hip Flexors
column16: Intermediate practitioners can perform with bent knees or one leg at a time. Beginners should build up with modified versions.
column17: https://example.com/videos/teaser.mp4

common_mistakes: Raising hips, arching back, not engaging core
description: Pilates Plank builds full-body strength with emphasis on core stabilization.
form_tips: Keep shoulders away from ears, engage abdominals to support spine, maintain a straight line from head to heels
instructions: 1. Begin in hands and knees position. 2. Step feet back to plank position, aligning shoulders over wrists. 3. Draw navel to spine and hold position with proper alignment. 4. Hold for 30-60 seconds, breathing normally.
name: Shoulders
column18: Beginners can perform from knees. Advanced practitioners can add leg lifts or arm reaches.

common_mistakes: Rushing the roll, using momentum, losing C-curve
description: Seal is an advanced rolling exercise that massages the spine, challenges balance, and requires deep core control.
form_tips: Maintain deep C-curve, clap feet 3 times in air, control the roll back and forth using abdominals
instructions: 1. Sit balanced on sit bones, knees bent wide, feet together. 2. Thread arms under legs, grasp outside of ankles. 3. Round spine into C-curve. 4. Inhale, roll back to shoulder blades, clapping feet 3 times. 5. Exhale, roll back up to balance without feet touching mat.
name: Spinal Flexors
column17: Intermediate can practice Rolling Like a Ball. Beginners should master basic core control first.

common_mistakes: Collapsing shoulder, hiking hip, using momentum
description: Side Bend prepares the body for more advanced side-lying work by strengthening the obliques and shoulder stabilizers.
form_tips: Lift from obliques not shoulder, keep hips stacked, maintain long line from head to feet
instructions: 1. Sit sideways with legs stacked or staggered, bottom hand supporting on mat. 2. Lift hips off mat, supporting weight on hand and feet/knee. 3. Reach top arm overhead. 4. Lower hips with control. Repeat other side.
name: Shoulders
column17: Beginners can perform from forearm or bent knee. Advanced can lift top leg or add rotation.

common_mistakes: Losing control, rushing sequence, improper transitions
description: Boomerang is a highly coordinated exercise linking Roll Over, Teaser, and arm circles, demanding core strength and fluidity.
form_tips: Maintain core engagement throughout, flow smoothly between positions, keep legs together
instructions: 1. Start seated like Roll Up end position. 2. Roll back, crossing ankles, legs parallel to floor (Roll Over). 3. Uncross ankles, roll up into Teaser position. 4. Lower legs, circle arms back, hinge forward. 5. Return to start.
name: Hip Flexors
column17: Requires mastery of Roll Over and Teaser. Practice components separately before combining.

common_mistakes: Wobbling, dropping leg, losing spinal length
description: Control Balance challenges core stability, balance, and flexibility as one leg extends overhead while the torso is inverted.
form_tips: Keep hips high and square, maintain length through spine, use core to stabilize not hands
instructions: 1. From Roll Over position, grasp one ankle. 2. Extend other leg straight towards ceiling. 3. Maintain balance on shoulders, using core control. 4. Switch legs with control.
name: Balance
column18: Requires strong Roll Over and core stabilization. Practice with support or reduced range initially.

common_mistakes: Using momentum, hunching shoulders, bending knees too much
description: Open Leg Rocker improves balance, core control, and hamstring flexibility while massaging the spine.
form_tips: Maintain lifted chest and long spine, balance on sit bones, control the rocking motion with abdominals
instructions: 1. Sit balanced holding ankles or calves, legs extended in V-shape. 2. Keep spine long, chest open. 3. Inhale, roll back to shoulder blades maintaining leg shape. 4. Exhale, roll back up to balance.
name: Balance
column18: Beginners can practice with bent knees or holding behind thighs. Advanced can keep legs straighter and closer together.

common_mistakes: Rounding from lower back, dropping head, pulling with arms
description: Spine Stretch Forward focuses on articulating the spine into forward flexion, stretching the back and hamstrings.
form_tips: Sit tall, initiate forward curve from upper spine, reach forward through crown of head not hands
instructions: 1. Sit tall with legs extended hip-width apart, feet flexed. 2. Inhale, lift arms forward parallel to floor. 3. Exhale, round spine forward vertebra by vertebra, reaching forward. 4. Inhale, stack spine back up to starting position.
name: Hamstrings
column17: Sit on cushion if hamstrings are tight. Focus on articulation rather than depth of stretch.

common_mistakes: Using momentum, losing core connection, lifting shoulders
description: Corkscrew challenges core control and spinal mobility through rotational leg circles from an inverted position.
form_tips: Keep shoulders anchored, initiate movement from core, control the circle size and speed
instructions: 1. Lie on back, legs extended to ceiling. 2. Inhale, circle legs to one side, down, around to other side, and back to center. 3. Exhale, reverse the circle. Keep hips stable initially, then add spinal rotation for advanced variation.
name: Obliques
column17: Beginners practice small circles with bent knees. Intermediate gradually increase circle size. Advanced add Roll Over entry/exit.

common_mistakes: Rocking pelvis, losing core engagement, limited range of motion
description: Hip Circles improve hip joint mobility and core stability, preparing for more advanced leg work.
form_tips: Keep pelvis stable on mat, use core to control leg movement, focus on smooth circular motion
instructions: 1. Lie on back, one knee bent with foot flat, other leg extended towards ceiling. 2. Stabilize pelvis. 3. Circle the extended leg across the body, down, around, and back up. 4. Perform circles in both directions. Repeat other leg.
name: Adductors
column18: Beginners make smaller circles or keep bottom leg bent. Advanced perform with both legs extended or add ankle weights.

common_mistakes: Lifting shoulders, dropping head, losing abdominal connection
description: Swimming Prep builds strength in the back extensors and glutes in a prone position, preparing for the full Swimming exercise.
form_tips: Keep neck long, lift from upper back not neck, reach long through limbs
instructions: 1. Lie prone, forehead resting on hands or mat, legs straight. 2. Engage core. 3. Inhale, lift head, chest, arms slightly off mat. 4. Exhale, lift opposite arm and leg slightly higher. 5. Inhale, lower. Exhale, lift other pair. Continue alternating.
name: Glutes
column17: Focus on length and control over height. Progress to lifting both arms/legs simultaneously before attempting full Swimming.

common_mistakes: Sinking in supporting hip, losing torso stability, kicking too high
description: Side Kick Kneeling strengthens the outer hip (gluteus medius) and core stabilizers from a challenging kneeling position.
form_tips: Keep supporting hand under shoulder, lift bottom ribs, maintain straight line from head to knee
instructions: 1. Kneel on one knee, other leg extended straight out to side. Place hand on floor under shoulder. 2. Lift extended leg to hip height. 3. Kick leg forward with flexed foot, then back with pointed foot. Maintain torso stability.
name: Obliques
column17: Beginners can place hand on hip instead of floor. Advanced can add circles or pulses with the leg.

common_mistakes: Dropping hips, arching back, flaring elbows wide
description: The Pilates Push Up integrates core stability with upper body strength, emphasizing control and proper form throughout.
form_tips: Maintain plank alignment, keep elbows close to body, lower and lift with control using core and arms
instructions: 1. Start standing, roll down vertebra by vertebra. 2. Walk hands out to plank position. 3. Perform 3-5 controlled push-ups, keeping elbows narrow. 4. Pike hips up, walk hands back towards feet. 5. Roll up to stand.
name: Triceps
column18: Beginners perform push-ups on knees. Advanced can add leg lifts during plank or increase push-up reps.

common_mistakes: Using neck rather than abdominals, losing C-curve, straining shoulders
description: Neck Pull is an advanced exercise that challenges abdominal control while articulating the spine without arm support.
form_tips: Initiate movement from abdominals not neck or arms, maintain C-curve throughout roll-up, keep shoulders away from ears
instructions: 1. Lie on back, legs extended, hands behind head. 2. Inhale to prepare. 3. Exhale, curl chin to chest and roll up (like Roll Up, but hands remain behind head). 4. Inhale at top. 5. Exhale, articulate spine down to mat.
name: Spinal Articulators
column17: Requires mastery of Roll Up. Practice with hands at sides before attempting with hands behind head.

common_mistakes: Lowering legs too far, collapsing chest, using momentum
description: Roll Over challenges core control and spinal flexibility in an inverted position, promoting circulation to the upper body.
form_tips: Keep core engaged to control movement, maintain even weight across shoulders (not neck), keep legs together
instructions: 1. Lie on back, legs extended to ceiling. 2. Inhale, lift hips, roll spine up and over, bringing legs overhead (but not touching floor). 3. Open legs to mat width. 4. Close legs. 5. Exhale, articulate spine down to mat.
name: Spinal Articulators
column17: Beginners can use bent knees or a smaller range of motion. Advanced can add pointed/flexed feet variations.

common_mistakes: Rushing movement, collapsing chest, using momentum
description: Jackknife combines core strength, balance, and control as the legs and torso form a V-shape with the body's weight on the shoulders.
form_tips: Keep shoulder blades stable on mat, engage core throughout, control both up and down phases
instructions: 1. Lie on back, arms by sides, legs extended to ceiling. 2. Roll hips up, supporting lower back with hands. 3. Extend legs straight up to ceiling, forming a straight line with torso. 4. Lower legs over head, then control roll down through spine.
name: Spinal Articulators
column17: Requires strong core and shoulder stabilizers. Master Roll Over before attempting.

common_mistakes: Compressing lower back, lifting shoulders, hyperextending neck
description: Swan strengthens the back extensors and opens the chest while teaching proper spinal extension.
form_tips: Press pubic bone into mat, lengthen through crown of head, use back muscles (not arms) to lift
instructions: 1. Lie prone, hands under shoulders, elbows close to body. 2. Press pubic bone into mat, engage abdominals. 3. Inhale, use back muscles to lift chest forward and up. 4. Exhale to lower with control.
name: Rhomboids
column17: Beginners can limit range of extension or use forearms. Advanced can progress to Swan Dive or add arm variations.

common_mistakes: Dropping chest, rounding shoulders, kicking with momentum
description: Single Leg Kick strengthens the hamstrings and glutes while maintaining upper body stability.
form_tips: Keep chest lifted, elbows directly under shoulders, engage abs to support lower back, kick with control
instructions: 1. Lie prone, upper body supported on forearms with elbows under shoulders. 2. Engage core and lift chest. 3. Pulse one heel toward buttock twice with a flexed foot. 4. Lower leg, switch sides. Continue alternating.
name: Glutes
column17: Beginners can keep chest lower. Advanced can increase tempo slightly while maintaining form.

common_mistakes: Collapsing chest, using arms instead of back, losing alignment
description: Double Leg Kick strengthens the entire posterior chain while promoting thoracic extension and shoulder mobility.
form_tips: Keep chest lifted, maintain length through the spine, coordinate breath with movement
instructions: 1. Lie prone, head turned to one side, hands clasped behind back. 2. Pulse both heels toward buttocks three times. 3. Lift chest and extend arms back and up. 4. Lower, turn head to other side. Repeat sequence.
name: Erector Spinae
column17: Beginners can keep hands on lower back instead of clasped. Advanced can lift higher in extension phase.

common_mistakes: Using momentum, rocking too fast, lifting head
description: Rocking gently massages the spine while strengthening the back extensors and deep core muscles.
form_tips: Maintain C-curve shape, keep movements fluid and controlled, breathe naturally throughout
instructions: 1. Start in a ball position on mat, knees drawn to chest. 2. Extend arms forward, palms down, parallel to floor. 3. Inhale as you roll back, maintaining ball shape. 4. Exhale as you roll forward, balancing momentarily. 5. Continue rocking rhythmically.
name: Back Extensors
column17: Beginners can hold knees for support. Advanced can extend legs slightly as they rock forward.

common_mistakes: Collapsing side waist, hiking hips, losing alignment
description: The Star is an advanced side-lying exercise that challenges full-body integration, stability, and balance.
form_tips: Create length through entire body, engage core throughout, maintain body in one plane
instructions: 1. Lie on side, body in straight line, supporting palm under shoulder. 2. Stack feet, engage core. 3. Press into palm, lift entire body off mat, extending top arm to ceiling. 4. Hold position with control, then lower.
name: Shoulder Stabilizers
column17: Master side plank first. Build progressively by starting with bent knees, then advancing to straight leg variations.

common_mistakes: Losing spinal length, twisting from shoulders, rounding lower back
description: Twist with Open Legs challenges rotational stability while maintaining a strong core and open hip position.
form_tips: Sit tall through spine before twisting, keep hips grounded, initiate rotation from waist
instructions: 1. Sit with legs spread wide, spine tall. 2. Extend arms to sides at shoulder height. 3. Inhale to prepare. 4. Exhale, rotate torso to one side. 5. Inhale to center. 6. Exhale, rotate to other side.
name: Spinal Rotators
column17: Beginners can reduce leg width. Advanced can add forward flexion after each rotation.

common_mistakes: Hunching shoulders, lifting ribs, not engaging upper back
description: Chest Expansion improves posture by strengthening the upper back and opening the chest, promoting better shoulder alignment.
form_tips: Draw shoulder blades down back, maintain height through crown of head, keep ribs connected to pelvis
instructions: 1. Kneel tall with knees hip-width apart. 2. Extend arms behind body, palms facing back. 3. Inhale, turn head to one side. 4. Exhale, pull arms back, opening chest. 5. Return head to center, switch sides.
name: Posterior Deltoids
column17: Can be performed seated or standing. Resistance band can be added for increased challenge.

common_mistakes: Dropping hips, sagging shoulders, collapsing chest
description: Leg Pull Back strengthens the entire body with emphasis on core, shoulders, glutes and hamstrings.
form_tips: Keep body in straight line, engage core throughout, maintain shoulders over wrists
instructions: 1. Start in reverse plank position, hands under shoulders, body in straight line. 2. Keep shoulders directly over wrists, feet flexed. 3. Lift one leg to hip height without shifting hips. 4. Lower and repeat, then switch legs.
name: Shoulder Stabilizers
column18: Beginners can practice regular plank first. Intermediate can hold position without leg lifts.

common_mistakes: Straining neck, collapsing chest, using momentum
description: Crab is an advanced exercise that combines a backward roll with arm support, challenging coordination and strength.
form_tips: Keep movements controlled, coordinate breath with movement, maintain C-curve when appropriate
instructions: 1. Start seated, knees bent, feet flat, hands behind shoulders with fingers pointing toward hips. 2. Lift hips into table top position. 3. Roll back, extending legs overhead. 4. Roll forward, returning to tabletop position. 5. Lower to start position.
name: Shoulder Stabilizers
column17: Master shoulder bridge and rolling exercises first. Use controlled movements throughout.

common_mistakes: Losing torso stability, lifting shoulders, losing alignment
description: Hip Twist with Stretched Arms challenges oblique strength and coordination while maintaining a stable upper body.
form_tips: Keep shoulders down, spine long, initiate movement from deep core muscles
instructions: 1. Sit tall, legs extended and together at 45 degrees, arms stretched forward at shoulder height. 2. Keeping torso stable, circle both legs together to one side, down, across, and back up. 3. Reverse direction. Maintain stability throughout.
name: Transverse Abdominis
column17: Beginners can bend knees slightly. Advanced can lower legs closer to mat during circles.

common_mistakes: Rounding from lower back, hunching shoulders, forcing rotation
description: Spine Stretch with Rotation combines forward flexion with spinal rotation, enhancing mobility and lateral flexibility.
form_tips: Sit tall before initiating movement, lengthen through crown of head, rotate from waist not shoulders
instructions: 1. Begin in Spine Stretch position (seated, legs extended wide, arms forward). 2. Inhale to prepare. 3. Exhale, roll down through spine, reaching forward. 4. At bottom of stretch, rotate torso to one side. 5. Return to center, roll up. 6. Repeat to other side.
name: Spinal Rotators
column17: Master basic Spine Stretch Forward first. Keep reach forward before adding rotation.

common_mistakes: Overarching lower back, squeezing glutes, lifting shoulders
description: Shoulder Bridge strengthens the posterior chain while mobilizing the spine through articulated movement.
form_tips: Press into feet evenly, roll spine one vertebra at a time, keep knees parallel throughout
instructions: 1. Lie on back with knees bent, feet flat, arms along sides. 2. Inhale to prepare. 3. Exhale, tuck pelvis and roll spine off mat vertebra by vertebra until weight is on shoulders. 4. Inhale at top. 5. Exhale, articulate spine down to mat.
name: Hamstrings
column17: Beginners focus on proper pelvic placement. Intermediate can add leg extensions. Advanced can perform with arms overhead.

common_mistakes: Winging scapulae, sinking chest, using momentum
description: Push Up to Side Plank combines upper body strength with rotational stability for a full-body challenge.
form_tips: Stack joints (shoulders over wrists, hips over ankles), engage core before rotation, maintain neutral spine
instructions: 1. Begin in plank position, hands under shoulders. 2. Lower into push-up. 3. Press up, shifting weight to one arm while rotating torso and extending opposite arm to ceiling. 4. Return to plank and repeat on other side.
name: Triceps
column18: Intermediate can perform from knees. Master push-up and side plank separately before combining.

common_mistakes: Jutting chin, hunching back, hiking shoulders
description: Forward Spine Stretch on Elbows improves spinal articulation while maintaining stability in the shoulder girdle.
form_tips: Draw shoulder blades down back, maintain connection from ribs to pelvis, keep navel drawn in
instructions: 1. Sit tall with legs extended wide, place forearms on mat in front of you, elbows under shoulders. 2. Inhale to prepare. 3. Exhale, curl spine forward leading with crown of head. 4. Inhale at full extension. 5. Exhale, roll back up to tall spine.
name: Shoulder Stabilizers
column17: Beginners can place elbows on elevated surface. Advanced can increase range by placing elbows further forward.

common_mistakes: Collapsing into passive stretch, rounding shoulders, dropping head
description: Mermaid with Rotation is a side-bending exercise that adds rotation to enhance thoracic mobility and lateral flexibility.
form_tips: Maintain length through both sides of torso, keep hips grounded, breathe into the stretching side
instructions: 1. Sit in Z-sit position (one leg bent in front, one behind). 2. Place hand on floor beside hip, other arm extended overhead. 3. Side-bend toward floor hand. 4. Add rotation of chest and arm upward. 5. Return to center and repeat.
name: Quadratus Lumborum
column17: Practice basic Mermaid first. Those with knee issues can sit cross-legged or with both legs to one side.

common_mistakes: Hyperextending knees, dropping head, shortening side waist
description: Side Splits enhances hamstring flexibility, hip mobility, and core control while moving between wide leg positions.
form_tips: Keep spine neutral, engage core throughout, distribute weight evenly between sitting bones
instructions: 1. Sit tall with legs extended in V-position. 2. Inhale, articulate forward from hips keeping spine long. 3. Exhale, close legs together. 4. Inhale, open legs to sides again. 5. Exhale, roll back up to tall spine.
name: Hamstrings
column17: Reduce range of motion if tight hamstrings. Add arm variations for increased challenge.

common_mistakes: Rocking pelvis, scrunching shoulders, kicking with momentum
description: Bicycle strengthens the abdominals and hip flexors while improving coordination and core control.
form_tips: Keep lower back pressed into mat, maintain stable pelvis, coordinate breath with movement
instructions: 1. Lie on back, hands behind head, elbows wide, knees over hips. 2. Extend one leg while rotating upper body to bring opposite elbow toward bent knee. 3. Switch sides in fluid bicycle pedaling motion, maintaining core engagement.
name: Obliques
column17: Beginners can keep movement smaller with knees higher. Advanced can extend legs lower.

common_mistakes: Breaking at waist, collapsing chest, bending elbows
description: Saw adds rotation to a forward bend, combining flexibility work with spinal mobility.
form_tips: Sit tall through spine before initiating movement, rotate from waist, reach through little finger
instructions: 1. Sit tall with legs extended wide, arms out to sides at shoulder height. 2. Rotate torso to one side. 3. Bend forward, reaching outside hand past opposite foot like a saw. 4. Roll up and repeat to other side.
name: Obliques
column17: Bend knees if hamstrings are tight. Emphasize rotation before bending forward.

common_mistakes: Crunching neck, using momentum, lifting shoulders
description: Criss Cross is a targeted oblique exercise that combines rotation with a classic abdominal crunch.
form_tips: Initiate rotation from waist not shoulders, maintain stable pelvis, keep elbows wide
instructions: 1. Lie on back with hands behind head, knees bent over hips. 2. Lift head and shoulders off mat. 3. Rotate upper body, bringing right elbow toward left knee as right leg extends. 4. Switch sides in controlled manner.
name: Rectus Abdominis
column17: Beginners can keep both knees bent, rotating torso only. Advanced can extend legs lower.

common_mistakes: Lifting shoulders, using momentum, straining neck
description: Heel Beats strengthens hamstrings and glutes in a prone position while maintaining upper body stability.
form_tips: Keep upper body stable, engage core throughout, use control not momentum
instructions: 1. Lie prone with forehead on stacked hands, legs straight. 2. Engage abdominals, lift both legs slightly off mat. 3. Beat heels together and apart in quick, controlled pulses. 4. Maintain neutral spine throughout.
name: Gluteal Complex
column17: Beginners keep legs lower. Advanced can increase leg height or duration of exercise.

common_mistakes: Losing pointed feet, breaking at waist, collapsing chest
description: Scissors challenges core strength and hip flexibility through controlled leg exchanges in an inverted position.
form_tips: Support lower back with hands, maintain stable shoulder base, coordinate breath with movement
instructions: 1. Lie on back, lift legs and hips, supporting lower back with hands. 2. Scissor legs, bringing one toward face and extending other toward ceiling. 3. Pulse twice, then switch leg positions with control. 4. Continue alternating.
name: Hip Flexors
column17: Requires strong core and shoulder stability. Master other inverted exercises first.

common_mistakes: Swaying torso, tucking pelvis, hyperextending knees
description: Standing Roll Down improves spinal articulation and hamstring flexibility while teaching proper hip hinging.
form_tips: Initiate movement from head, roll down vertebra by vertebra, keep weight centered over feet
instructions: 1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, arms at sides. 2. Inhale to prepare. 3. Exhale, nod chin and roll down through spine vertebra by vertebra. 4. Inhale at bottom, allowing knees to bend if needed. 5. Exhale, roll back up to standing.
name: Hamstrings
column17: Bend knees as needed for tight hamstrings. Advanced can add arm variations or perform near wall.

common_mistakes: Holding breath, gripping neck, using momentum
description: Curl Up is a focused abdominal exercise that teaches proper spinal flexion and core activation.
form_tips: Draw navel to spine, keep neck long, focus on abdominal connection
instructions: 1. Lie on back with knees bent, feet flat, hands behind head. 2. Inhale to prepare. 3. Exhale, curl upper body off mat leading with chest (not chin). 4. Inhale, lower with control.
name: Transverse Abdominis
column16: Emphasis on quality over quantity. Advanced can add rotation or leg extensions.
column17: https://example.com/videos/curl_up.mp4

common_mistakes: Lifting shoulders, straining neck, losing form over time
description: Spine Corrector Series combines spinal extension, flexion, and rotation on a specialized arc-shaped apparatus or foam roller.
form_tips: Maintain connection between ribs and pelvis, focus on articulating through each vertebra, breathe into movement
instructions: 1. Begin seated on apex of spine corrector, knees bent. 2. Roll back over arc, extending spine. 3. Return to seated. 4. Perform variations including side bends, rotations, and extensions as prescribed.
name: Spinal Flexors
column17: Can be modified using a foam roller. Adapt range of motion based on spinal flexibility.

common_mistakes: Shifting weight unevenly, gripping with toes, compromising alignment
description: Foot Exercises strengthen the intrinsic muscles of the feet while improving balance and alignment from the ground up.
form_tips: Focus on articulation through toes and arches, maintain ankle stability, keep rest of body aligned
instructions: 1. Sit tall or stand with good posture. 2. Perform sequence of foot exercises: toe spreading, point-flex, ankle circles, towel gathering, and marble pickup. 3. Focus on isolation and controlled movement.
name: Tibialis Anterior
column17: Can be performed seated or standing. Add resistance with bands or weights for progression.

common_mistakes: Sinking in lower back, protruding ribs, bending knees too much
description: Wall Series uses the wall as feedback for proper alignment while strengthening the core and improving posture.
form_tips: Press entire back against wall, maintain neutral pelvis, keep shoulders relaxed
instructions: 1. Stand with back against wall, feet hip-width apart and slightly forward. 2. Perform exercises including wall rolls, arm circles, heel raises, and small squats. 3. Maintain contact with wall throughout for alignment feedback.
name: Postural Muscles
column17: Excellent for beginners and those with back issues. Progress by adding resistance or balance challenges.

common_mistakes: Breaking wrists, sinking in shoulders, popping ribs
description: Plank Variations challenge core strength and full-body integration through multiple plank positions and transitions.
form_tips: Keep shoulders over wrists, engage abdominals throughout, maintain long line from head to heels
instructions: 1. Begin in standard plank position. 2. Progress through variations including: forearm plank, side plank, plank with leg lift, and plank with arm reach. 3. Focus on stability and control during transitions.
name: Shoulder Stabilizers
column17: Modify by dropping to knees for each variation. Advanced can add dynamic movements or unstable surfaces.

common_mistakes: Tucking pelvis, locking knees, gripping shoulders
description: Pelvic Curl is a fundamental exercise that teaches proper pelvic-lumbar articulation and core activation.
form_tips: Initiate movement from tailbone, articulate through each vertebra, maintain width across collarbones
instructions: 1. Lie on back with knees bent, feet flat, arms along sides. 2. Inhale to prepare. 3. Exhale, tilt pelvis to flatten lower back, then continue rolling up vertebra by vertebra to bridge position. 4. Inhale at top. 5. Exhale, roll down one vertebra at a time.
name: Glutes
column17: Focus on articulation rather than height. Great preparation for more advanced bridging exercises.

common_mistakes: Hiking shoulders, holding breath, moving too quickly
description: Push Through is a classical Pilates exercise typically performed on the Cadillac but adaptable to mat work with modifications.
form_tips: Keep shoulder blades stable, engage abdominals throughout, coordinate breath with movement
instructions: 1. Lie on back with legs in tabletop position. 2. Extend arms toward ceiling. 3. Simultaneously extend legs forward and arms overhead, creating a long line. 4. Return to starting position with control.
name: Lats
column17: On mat, beginners can keep knees bent. On Cadillac, work with instructor for proper spring tension.

common_mistakes: Losing hip alignment, arching lower back, using momentum
description: Grasshopper is an advanced prone exercise that challenges back strength, hamstring flexibility, and balance.
form_tips: Press pubic bone into mat, engage abdominals to support lower back, reach through fingertips and toes
instructions: 1. Lie prone with arms extended overhead. 2. Simultaneously lift arms, chest, and legs off mat. 3. Hold position, then add small rocking movement forward and back. 4. Maintain length and control throughout.
name: Gluteal Complex
column17: Master swan and swimming before attempting. Support lower back with pillow under hips if needed.

common_mistakes: Hyperextending knees, collapsing through upper back, losing balance
description: Standing Leg Pumps strengthen the lower body while challenging balance and core stability in an upright position.
form_tips: Maintain vertical alignment, engage standing leg fully, keep hips level
instructions: 1. Stand tall with weight on one leg. 2. Extend other leg forward, slightly off floor. 3. Pump extended leg up and down in small, controlled movements. 4. Maintain stable upper body throughout. 5. Repeat on other side.
name: Hamstrings
column17: Use wall or chair for support if needed. Advanced can add arm movements or close eyes.

common_mistakes: Rounding shoulders, jutting chin, losing core engagement
description: Seated Twist with Leg Extension combines spinal rotation with leg articulation for a comprehensive core challenge.
form_tips: Sit tall before initiating twist, keep shoulders down, extend leg with control
instructions: 1. Sit tall with knees bent, feet flat. 2. Place hands behind head, elbows wide. 3. Rotate torso to one side. 4. Simultaneously extend opposite leg. 5. Return to center and repeat on other side.
name: Rectus Abdominis
column17: Beginners can keep both feet on floor. Advanced can lift extended leg higher.

common_mistakes: Using momentum, losing spinal alignment, hunching shoulders
description: Thread the Needle is a rotational exercise that targets the obliques while providing a gentle stretch for the shoulders and upper back.
form_tips: Start in stable quadruped position, move with control, breathe into rotation
instructions: 1. Begin in quadruped position (hands and knees). 2. Extend one arm to the side, then up toward ceiling. 3. Rotate torso, threading arm under body and reaching to opposite side. 4. Return to extended position, then repeat on other side.
name: Spinal Rotators
column17: Focus on rotation rather than stretch. Can be modified by performing from forearms for wrist sensitivity.
